title: Polyamine Buffer for Bacterial Artificial Chromosome (BAC) DNA
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2sdre","text":"Abstract","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7cobf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2tifk","text":"Bacterial Artificial Chromosomes (BAC) can include over 300 kb of DNA while Yeast Artificial Chromosomes (YAC) can include as much as 1 Mb of DNA. Such long DNA molecules are prone to fragmentation when stored in standard DNA resuspension buffers such as 10 mM Tris, 1 mM EDTA (Montoliu et al. 1995, Van Keuren et al. 2009). To protect long DNA molecules from fragmentation so that they can be used as templates for recombineering (Zeidler and Saunders, 2019) or as transgenes in the production of transgenic mice (Said et al. 2019) and transgenic rats (Witten et al. 2011).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"adu53","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"91p96","text":"Montoliu L, Bock CT, Schütz G, Zentgraf H. 1995. Visualization of large DNA molecules by electron microscopy with polyamines: application to the analysis of yeast endogenous and artificial chromosomes. J Mol Biol. 246:486-92.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1n1ue","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3nerv","text":"Said R, Zheng L, Saunders T, Zeidler M, Papagerakis S, Papagerakis P. 2019. Transgenic Res. 18:769-85. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e1vc7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bm4ag","text":"Witten IB, Steinberg EE, Lee SY, Davidson TJ, Zalocusky KA, Brodsky M, Yizhar O, Cho SL, Gong S, Ramakrishnan C, Stuber GD, Tye KM, Janak PH, Deisseroth K. 2011. steps: A B C D E 1 Reagent Source Cat. No. Stock Concentration Final Concentration 2 Tris-HCl, pH 7.4 SigmaAldrich 93313 1 M 0.01 M 3 EDTA SigmaAldrich E7889 500 mM 0.01 mM 4 Spermine Tetrahydrochloride SigmaAldrich S-1141 (1g) See 1000X polyamine stock 30 uM 5 Spermidine Trihydrochloride SigmaAldrich S-2501 (5g) See 1000X polyamine stock 70 uM 6 NaCl SigmaAldrich S5150 5 M 100 uM 7 Water for DNA SigmaAldrich W4502 8 Sterile 0.22 micron filters 9 Sterile tubes various sizes 10 List of Materials for polyamine buffer
Prepare Polyamine Microinjection Buffer A B 1 Solution Volume 2 1 M Tris-HCl, pH7.4 0.5 ml 3 0.5 M EDTA 0.01 ml 4 5 M NaCl 1.0 ml 5 1000X Polyamine Stock 0.05 ml 6 Water for DNA 48.44 ml 7

Resuspend DNA in polyamine buffer and quantitate with your preferred method. It may be necessary to allow DNA pellets to enter solution during an overnight incubation in the refrigerator. Store DNA at 4°C. DO NOT STORE RESUSPENDED DNA IN THE FREEZER.

Prepare 1000X polyamine stock. Polyamine powders are extremely hygroscopic. It is recommended that small quantities be ordered and used entirely in the preparation of a 1000X Polyamine stock solution. Dissolve 0.94 g spermine and 3 g spermidine together in 123.0 ml sterile water for DNA. Then filter sterilize through 0.22 micrometer filtration. Prepare aliquots and store at -20°C.
creator: Thom Saunders
